expectations.evidence: The conversation states the agent 'Used spike template with title starting 'Spike:''.

expectations.text: The draft uses the spike template structure with Background, Current Knowledge, Scope, Outcomes, and Deliverables.
expectations.evidence: The conversation notes the draft includes 'Background, Current Knowledge, Scope, Outcomes' but does not mention a Deliverables section, so only 4 of the 5 required sections are present.

expectations.text: The response references modularisation.md together with current notification implementation files such as NotificationsScreen, NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsOrchestrator, or data/notification classes.
expectations.evidence: The conversation lists many concrete notification implementation files (NotificationsScreen.kt, NotificationsViewModel.kt, NotificationsOrchestrator.kt, etc.) but does not mention modularisation.md at all.

expectations.text: The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
expectations.evidence: The conversation states 'Stopped at draft/approval mode' and no issue or body file was created.

expectations.text: The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.
expectations.evidence: The conversation states 'Title not duplicated in body'.

expectations.text: When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
expectations.evidence: No body file was written (agent stopped at draft/approval mode without creating the issue), so the condition is trivially satisfied.

suggestions: The draft must include all 5 spike template sections: Background, Current Knowledge, Scope, Outcomes, and Deliverables — Deliverables was missing.
The response should explicitly reference modularisation.md alongside the notification implementation files, as the prompt requested research 'as described in modularisation.md'.
eval_feedback.overall: 4 of 6 expectations passed. The agent correctly used the Spike: title prefix, stopped at draft/approval mode, avoided duplicating the title in the body, and did not write a code-fenced body file. However, the template was incomplete (missing Deliverables) and modularisation.md was not referenced.
